description_long: This object is a vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder, featuring two large black reels mounted on the front panel, one for supply and one for take-up. The front face is equipped with a variety of physical controls, including rotary knobs, toggle switches, sliders, and several buttons, as well as a small display and indicator lights. The casing is primarily constructed from dark metal and plastic, with ventilation grilles on the rear and a visible power cord for AC mains connection. The back panel includes additional labeling, ventilation, and a compartment for cable management. The overall design is boxy and robust, typical of mid-20th-century audio equipment, and the object is intended to rest on a flat surface such as a table or shelf. The device is likely used for audio recording and playback in professional or enthusiast settings.
description: A vintage reel-to-reel tape recorder with dual large reels, numerous physical controls, and a sturdy dark metal and plastic casing.
description_view_0: Front view showing two large reels, control panel with knobs, switches, sliders, buttons, and a small display.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the depth of the device, the reels' profile, and the protruding power cord.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing ventilation grilles, labeling, cable compartment, and the power cord connection.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the side profile and the power cord trailing out.
attribute_control_interface: physical buttons, rotary knobs, sliders, toggle switches
